questions.question: 1. Why can't standard integer types store numbers like 10^50?

answers.b: They are not used in programming

explanations.a: Incorrect. Integers do not store characters.
explanations.b: Incorrect. Integers are widely used in programming.
explanations.c: Correct. Standard types have fixed size and can't store very large numbers.
explanations.d: Incorrect. There is a maximum value for each type.

questions.question: 2. What is the main idea behind adding two large numbers stored as strings?

answers.a: Add digits from left to right only
answers.b: Convert strings to integers and add
answers.d: Add digits from right to left, carrying over as needed

explanations.a: Incorrect. Addition is typically done from right to left.
explanations.b: Incorrect. Large numbers can't be converted to standard integers.
explanations.d: Correct. Addition is done digit by digit from right to left, carrying over.

questions.question: 3. Which of the following is a valid reason to use binary search for square root calculation?

answers.b: It efficiently narrows down the possible values
answers.c: It is slower than linear search
answers.d: It only works for small numbers

explanations.a: Incorrect. Binary search does not check every value.
explanations.b: Correct. Binary search quickly reduces the search interval.
explanations.c: Incorrect. Binary search is faster than linear search.
explanations.d: Incorrect. Binary search works for large numbers too.

questions.question: 4. What is the time complexity of binary search for finding square roots?

explanations.b: Incorrect. Constant time is not possible for arbitrary N.
explanations.c: Incorrect. Quadratic time is too slow.
explanations.d: Correct. Each step halves the interval, so it's logarithmic.

questions.question: 5. Which of the following is NOT a challenge when adding large numbers as strings?

explanations.a: Incorrect. Large numbers may require more memory.
explanations.b: Incorrect. Carry over must be handled.
explanations.c: Incorrect. Digits must be aligned for addition.
explanations.d: Correct. Built-in addition can't be used for very large numbers.

questions.question: 6. What is the output of adding 999...999 (50 nines) and 1?

explanations.b: Incorrect. The sum is much larger.
explanations.c: Correct. Adding 1 to all nines results in a 1 followed by zeros.
explanations.d: Incorrect. The sum is not zero.

questions.question: 7. Which of the following is true about floating-point precision in square root calculation?

answers.b: Results may not be exact due to rounding

explanations.a: Incorrect. Results are not always exact.
explanations.b: Correct. Floating-point results are rounded to a certain precision.
explanations.c: Incorrect. Precision is important for accuracy.
explanations.d: Incorrect. Binary search works for floating-point values.

questions.question: 8. What is the main application of large number arithmetic in cryptography?

explanations.a: Correct. Cryptography relies on arithmetic with very large numbers.
explanations.b: Incorrect. Sorting is not specific to cryptography.
explanations.c: Incorrect. Maximum value is not the main application.
explanations.d: Incorrect. Printing is not the main application.

questions.question: 9. Which of the following is a limitation of using strings for arithmetic?

answers.a: Slower operations compared to built-in types

explanations.a: Correct. String-based arithmetic is slower than using integers.
explanations.b: Incorrect. Precision is limited by memory, not truly unlimited.
explanations.c: Incorrect. Carry over is still needed.
explanations.d: Incorrect. Type conversion is not automatic.

questions.question: 10. Which of the following is the best reason to use binary search for square root calculation instead of trial division?

answers.a: It is much faster for large numbers
answers.b: It always gives an exact answer
answers.d: It does not require any arithmetic

explanations.a: Correct. Binary search is efficient for large numbers.
explanations.b: Incorrect. It may not always be exact due to floating-point rounding.
explanations.c: Incorrect. Coding difficulty is not the main reason.
explanations.d: Incorrect. Arithmetic is required for the calculation.
